hero_tagline: Finding the perfect holiday park for your family along the Fraser Coast means discovering a place where your kids can make lifelong memories while you enjoy the relaxed Queensland coastal lifestyle together.
heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks on the Fraser Coast
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to the Fraser Coast? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks across the Fraser Coast so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
The Fraser Coast centres on Hervey Bay's calm water and whale season, with K'gari across the strait and a strong run of beachfront parks along the Esplanade.
local_knowledge: This region is effectively Hervey Bay plus its islands and hinterland, so the same rules apply: the bay is calm and shallow, whales rest here from July to October, and the Esplanade strip at Torquay and Scarness carries the daily rhythm of playgrounds, the pier and bike paths. Discovery Parks Fraser Street leads on facilities with two pools and go-karts, while Pialba Beach Tourist Park sits beside the free council WetSide water park. K'gari day tours and barges leave from River Heads. Whale watching cruises run daily in season and see close encounters with calving humpbacks most trips.

if_we_were_booking.label: Facilities and security
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Discovery Parks Fraser Street for the two heated pools, go-karts and games room near Torquay Beach.

if_we_were_booking.label: Free water park next door
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Pialba Beach Tourist Park for direct beach access plus the adjoining council WetSide water play area at no extra cost.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Book a whale watching cruise if you're here July to October, it's the best in the country, then spend the rest of the stay on the Esplanade with bikes and the WetSide splash zones.

faq.question: Is the Fraser Coast good for families?
faq.answer: Yes, it's built around calm bay water, whale season and beachfront parks with an easy day-trip island next door.

faq.question: Which Fraser Coast park is best for kids?
faq.answer: Discovery Parks Fraser Street, with two heated pools, go-karts, tennis and a games room, a short walk from Torquay Beach.

faq.question: When can you see whales?
faq.answer: July to October, when humpbacks rest in Hervey Bay with their calves. It's recognised as one of the best whale watching regions in the world.

faq.question: What is WetSide?
faq.answer: A free council water park on the Esplanade at Pialba, with paid slides and free splash areas. Pialba Beach Tourist Park sits right beside it.

faq.question: How do you get to K'gari from here?
faq.answer: Barges and day tours leave from River Heads, about 15 minutes from the main beach strip.

faq.question: Is the swimming safe for young kids?
faq.answer: Yes, the bay is calm, shallow and largely surf-free, which makes Torquay and Scarness among the safest family beaches in Queensland.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: The Esplanade bike path, WetSide's free zones, fishing off Urangan Pier and the Saturday markets at Torquay.

faq.question: How far is the Fraser Coast from Brisbane?
faq.answer: About 3.5 hours drive, making it one of the easier big family trips from the south east.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: The park games rooms, the Fraser Coast Discovery Sphere and the cinema at Pialba cover wet days.

faq.question: How far ahead should you book?
faq.answer: Whale season overlapping school holidays, July to September, books out the beachfront parks well ahead.
